text_block_list.name: Aston Machan
text_block_list.text:
Sumo wrestler food is all chanko hot pot, right?
So if I make hot pot, do you think I should call it
"ma-chanko hot pot"?

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Ma-chanko hot pot! Sounds great! What kind of
hot pot is it though? You have to tell me!

text_block_list.name: Aston Machan
text_block_list.text:
First, you put fruit, lemon juice, and cane sugar in
a pot and let them battle it out until they blend
together. You'll know it's done when it looks like
a fox.

text_block_list.name: Aston Machan
text_block_list.text:
Then you take flour, sugar, and chilled butter and
squeeze it all up like you're giving your grandpa a
shoulder rub.

text_block_list.name: Aston Machan
text_block_list.text:
Then, let that mingle in the long-neglected pot
for a bit and send them all on vacation to a hot
summer oven until golden brown.

text_block_list.name: Aston Machan
text_block_list.text:
There you have it. That's ma-chanko hot pot. It's
a pick-me dish that plays nice with both custard
and ice cream.

text_block_list.name: Hishi Akebono
text_block_list.text:
Just like apple crumble—sounds delicious! I get it.
If it's hot and in a pot, then that counts as hot pot
cooking! ♪

text_block_list.name: Aston Machan
text_block_list.text:
Do you think it'd be popular? I wonder if
ma-chanko hot pot would sell well.
